case_name: Esegbona v King's College Hospital NHS Foundation Trust (false imprisonment in hospital) [2019] EWHC 77 (QB) (22 January 2019)

summary.brief: Medical malpractice case involving obstetrics gynaecology.

evidence_index.text: Mrs Esegbona attended the accident and emergency department of King's College Hospital in the evening of 19th October 2010 with shortness of breath and was admitted in the early hours of 20th October.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: She was found to have pulmonary oedema caused by acute left-sided heart failure in the context of fast paroxysmal atrial fibrillation.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: She suffered a further severe hypoglycaemic episode on 28th October at around 3:45pm during which she had a seizure and aspirated.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: A psychiatric review on 7th April found that Mrs Esegbona did not have capacity to make the decision to have a CT angiogram.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: On 23rd June 2011 Mrs Esegbona sadly died. She had been found by staff at Wilsmere House unresponsive and with her tracheostomy tube removed.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Mr Olarinde and Dr Vletsi agree that if Wilsmere House was not informed that the deceased had previously been found with her tracheostomy tube out on 8th March, that would amount to breach of duty.
evidence_index.speaker: Expert testimony

evidence_index.text: I find that Mrs Esegbona was unlawfully imprisoned from the date of Dr Bhavsar's recommendation on 15 th February until her discharge to Wilsmere House on 14 th June, a total of 119 days.
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's conclusion

evidence_index.text: I accept Lynne Phair's evidence that she would not have remained on an acute hospital ward for as long as she did.
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's analysis

evidence_index.text: I think that this is a straightforward 'but for' case in terms of causation.
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's analysis

evidence_index.text: There will be judgement for the claimant in the sums identified above.
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's conclusion

patient_demographics.relevant_social_history: English not first language, lived independently

initial_state.history_of_present_illness: Presented with shortness of breath, found to have pulmonary oedema due to acute left-sided heart failure in the context of fast paroxysmal atrial fibrillation.

reveal.result_summary: Small left cerebellar and pons infarcts
Patient lacked capacity to make decisions about tracheostomy and future residence
reveal.clinical_significance: Requires best interests meeting and possible Court of Protection referral
reveal.decision_impact: Should have prompted legal and ethical considerations

timeline_phases.name: Initial Presentation
timeline_phases.description: Patient presents to ED with acute symptoms

timeline_phases.name: Discharge Planning
timeline_phases.description: Planning for patient's discharge from hospital

timeline_phases.name: Care Management Decision
timeline_phases.description: Decisions regarding level of care and supervision

decision_points.clinical_context: Patient ready for discharge from hospital
decision_points.prompt: What information should be provided to the nursing home?

options.description: Provide detailed tracheostomy care instructions and history of self-extubation
options.clinical_reasoning: Ensures nursing home is prepared for potential complications

options.description: Provide basic discharge summary without detailed tracheostomy history
options.clinical_reasoning: Fails to prepare nursing home for specific care needs

expected_action_court.description: Detailed tracheostomy care instructions should have been provided
expected_action_court.standard_of_care_basis: Expert evidence indicated necessity for detailed information

explanation.why_defendant_wrong: Failure to provide detailed care instructions increased risk of adverse events
explanation.what_should_have_happened: Detailed instructions would have prepared nursing home staff

decision_points.clinical_context: Patient's capacity to make decisions about discharge
decision_points.prompt: What should be done regarding the patient's expressed wish to go home?

options.description: Refer to Court of Protection for decision on discharge
options.clinical_reasoning: Ensures legal and ethical compliance with patient's rights

options.description: Proceed with discharge to nursing home without Court of Protection referral
options.clinical_reasoning: Disregards patient's expressed wishes and legal requirements

actual_action_defendant.description: Defendant discharged patient to nursing home
actual_action_defendant.reasoning_stated: Believed it was in patient's best interest

expected_action_court.description: Referral to Court of Protection should have been made
expected_action_court.standard_of_care_basis: Legal requirement under Mental Capacity Act

explanation.why_defendant_wrong: Failure to refer to Court of Protection violated patient's rights
explanation.what_should_have_happened: Court of Protection would have assessed best interests

patient_outcome.description: Patient died due to cardiac arrest following tracheostomy tube removal

key_findings: Causation established - lack of supervision led to tube removal and cardiac arrest

malpractice_determination.point_of_failure: Failure to provide one-to-one nursing care
malpractice_determination.counterfactual: Proper supervision would have prevented tube removal

factual_timeline.event: Patient admitted to hospital with shortness of breath
Patient presented with pulmonary oedema and heart failure
Patient admitted to hospital with heart failure

tests_performed.result: Small left cerebellar and pons infarcts

diagnoses.diagnosis: Pulmonary oedema due to acute left-sided heart failure
Pulmonary oedema due to acute heart failure

treatments.treatment: Hospital admission for heart failure management

complications.complication: Cardiac arrest due to tracheostomy tube removal

allegations.allegation: Failure to provide adequate discharge information
Failure to refer to Court of Protection

court_findings.finding: Defendant breached duty of care by not involving Court of Protection
